turns.content: I've been trying to slice strings in Rust but I keep hitting weird encoding issues. Can you explain why indexing &str by byte is unsafe?

That's a great question because it trips up most beginners. The issue is that &str represents UTF-8 encoded text where characters can span multiple bytes, so treating it as a simple byte array breaks on multi-byte sequences like emojis or accented letters. For example if you have the string "café" and try to access index 2 you get 'é' which takes two bytes but slicing by byte position returns only part of that character.
So I should just avoid indexing &str entirely? But what if I really need to extract specific characters or substrings?

You don't have to avoid it completely but you must use the right tools. Instead of byte indexing you should use char_indices which returns iterators that give you the character position and index in the string. This ensures you always work with complete characters rather than raw bytes.
Wait so char_indices is safer but how do I actually get a substring from it? It feels like there's still some way to break apart UTF-8 sequences if not careful.

Exactly the concern. When using char_indices you get byte offsets that are guaranteed to be at character boundaries so any slice created from those positions is safe. You can combine it with range syntax like .get(start..end) or manual indexing on the &str slice itself since both endpoints will always align with character starts and ends.
